Epithelial Systems Biology Laboratory (ESBL)


Comprehensive Proteomic Analysis of Rat Renal Inner Medullary Collecting Duct Cytoplasmic Fraction

This database reports proteins identified by mass spectrometry (LC-MS/MS) on the basis of at least two distinct peptides in the cytoplasmic fraction of inner medullary collecting duct (IMCD) cells freshly isolated from rat kidneys (NE-PER nuclear isolation kit, Thermo). Two samples were analyzed, one from IMCD cells treated for 30 minutes with a vasopressin analog (dDAVP, 1 nM) and one from IMCD cells treated with the vehicle (Control). This database was created by Cameron Grady, Christina Pickering, Chung-Lin Chou, Pablo Sandoval, and Mark Knepper of the Systems Biology Center, Division of Intramural Research, NHLBI, NIH, Bethesda, Maryland. Reference: LeMaire SM, Raghuram V, Grady CR, Pickering CM, Chou CL, Umejiego EN, Knepper MA. Serine/threonine phosphatases and aquaporin-2 regulation in renal collecting duct. Am J Physiol Renal Physiol. 2017 Jan 1;312: F84-F95. PMID: 27784696.

The p-values represent the probability that a difference in spectral counts between control and dDAVP samples could have been obtained by random selection from all spectra found in the respective fraction (Fisher Exact Test).

Download data here. Please address questions to knep@helix.nih.gov
